briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — finesse pre-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for pre-spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Trim the skirt and add a narrow shad profile soft plastic trailer to improve visual attraction and hooking percentages; bass take the bait deeper and hold on longer than with a skirt alone
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'I pick up my fishing pace considerably as the water hits around 52°F. I may run down a bank quickly throwing a lipless crankbait or spinnerbait, then pause to pitch a jig at submerged logs or brushpiles.'
Wheeler: At Santee Cooper, pre-spawn keys were 10 ft and shallower, hard places and rock (hard bottom, rock, gravel), plus brush and stumps. Better if you had both rock and wood but didn't need both

Palaniuk: 'I call it floating a crankbait -- pick a crankbait that dives deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel it so it crawls up and over rocks, then back down the other side. It looks like a crawfish slowly scooting.'
Robertson: In Florida grass (pencil reeds, flat reeds, Kissimmee grass, hyacinth), drop the trolling motor and cover water with swimbaits rather than sitting on one spot.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal pre-spawn fish pull back to 6-12ft staging areas. They won't commit shallow after the front. Target the first breakline off spawning flats and key on deeper structure near shallow spawning zones.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
